justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVEs flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers, all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string ESM, left-pad supply chain, moment deprecation), safe_to_deploy is false, and severities are well-differentiated (Critical, High, Important, Suggestion levels).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with concrete example of breaking change (v13→v14 charges.create removal)
jsonwebtoken flagged with specific CVE numbers (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540) and clear upgrade path to >=9.0.0
crypto-random-string ESM-only issue correctly identified with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM explanation and CJS assumption justified
left-pad supply chain risk called out with historical context (2016 incident) and native alternative (padStart)
moment.js deprecation noted with appropriate lower severity than the security issues
Bonus finding: missing package-lock.json as a build reproducibility risk, which strengthens the overall review
Severity differentiation is excellent — Critical for stripe/jwt, High for lockfile, Important for moment/left-pad/ESM, Suggestions for hardening

jsonwebtoken flagged as Critical with four specific CVE numbers and reproduction steps

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with clear explanation of payment processing risks
jsonwebtoken CVE-2022-23529 specifically named with RCE details — exceeds expectations
